summary: Russian forces are actively intercepting Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs using the "Yolka" kinetic anti-drone system, with multiple successful intercepts reported in spring 2026. The "Rubikon" Center conducted intensive operations on March 12 and 13, targeting Ukrainian personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and control points, destroying 111 and 132 targets respectively across Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. Ukraine is significantly increasing multirotor drone procurement, with deliveries in January–February 2026 more than doubling year-over-year. On the tactical front, Ukrainian FPV pilots are experimenting with tactics like destabilizing Russian infantry before strikes, while Russian forces are deploying FPV detectors and facing novel threats such as coordinated multi-drone attacks and direct infantry-drone engagements.
